skill.name: receiving-code-review
skill.description: Guides AI coding agents to evaluate code review feedback before changing code, with emphasis on clarification, verification, technical pushback, and incremental fixes.
skill.summary: Evaluate code review feedback before implementing changes.
skill.author: obra

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown code fences as Ruby backtick execution and matched benign text as weak cryptography and network reconnaissance. Manual review found instructional Markdown only, with no scripts, executable code, credential access, automatic network activity, or prompt injection attempts. The skill includes user-initiated grep and gh api guidance, so the residual publication risk is low.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misidentified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The 24 Ruby backtick execution alerts point to Markdown code fence delimiters and example text, not executable shell or Ruby code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each alert maps to Markdown examples or code fence delimiters in SKILL.md. No executable Ruby, shell script, or automatic command runner is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Benign Text Matches
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported locations contain front matter description and example response wording, not cryptographic functions or algorithm use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no hashing, encryption, crypto libraries, or algorithm selection. The static pattern lacks semantic support.

low_findings.title: Network Reconnaissance Alert Is Benign Guidance
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported line advises technical reasoning when responding to review feedback and does not instruct network scanning.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line contains no host discovery, port scanning, or network probing instruction. No network reconnaissance intent appears elsewhere in the skill.

content.user_title: Evaluate Code Review Feedback With Rigor
content.value_statement: Code review feedback can be unclear, incomplete, or technically wrong. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to verify suggestions before changing code.
actual_capabilities: Separates understanding, verification, response, and implementation steps for review feedback.
Prompts clarification before partial work on unclear multi-item feedback.
Encourages checking reviewer suggestions against codebase behavior, compatibility, and prior decisions.
Provides pushback patterns for technically incorrect or out-of-scope suggestions.
Defines implementation order for blocking, simple, and complex fixes.
Explains how to reply to GitHub inline review threads.
limitations: Does not execute commands or make code changes by itself.
Depends on repository access, tests, and review context for verification.
Does not replace human decisions on architecture or product scope.
Contains style guidance that may conflict with teams that prefer polite acknowledgments.

use_cases.title: Triage Pull Request Feedback
use_cases.description: Evaluate each review comment before editing, clarify unclear items, and choose a safe implementation order.

use_cases.title: Coach AI Coding Agents
use_cases.description: Guide Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to avoid blind agreement and verify suggestions against the codebase.

use_cases.title: Resolve Conflicting Review Advice
use_cases.description: Compare reviewer suggestions with existing behavior, compatibility needs, and prior product decisions.

prompt_templates.title: Clarify a Review Comment
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner triage for one unclear comment.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use receiving-code-review. I received this review comment: [paste comment]. Restate the requested change and list questions I should ask before editing.

prompt_templates.title: Verify Before Editing
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of a concrete suggestion.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use receiving-code-review. Check whether this suggestion is correct for the current codebase: [paste suggestion]. Identify files, tests, or behavior to inspect before implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Multi-Item Review Fixes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use receiving-code-review. Here are review items 1 through 6: [paste list]. Separate unclear items, order the remaining fixes, and define validation for each change.

prompt_templates.title: Draft Technical Pushback
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced response when a suggestion may be wrong.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use receiving-code-review. A reviewer suggested [change]. The codebase context is [context]. Draft a concise technical response if the suggestion is incorrect or risky.

output_examples.input: Reviewer says to remove a legacy fallback.
output: Check supported platform targets before removing it.
Verify whether tests cover the fallback path.
If backward compatibility is still required, explain the constraint and propose a smaller fix.

output_examples.input: A human partner says to fix items 1 through 6, but items 4 and 5 are unclear.
output: Ask for clarification on items 4 and 5 before implementing any part of the batch.
Do not split related review feedback without understanding the full scope.

output_examples.input: Reviewer asks for a large professional metrics feature.
output: Search for current callers or product requirements.
If unused, ask whether the endpoint should be removed instead.
If used, implement only the behavior required by existing callers.

best_practices: Read all feedback before responding or editing.
Verify each suggestion against actual code, tests, platform support, and ownership decisions.
Fix one item at a time and run targeted validation after each change.
anti_patterns: Agreeing enthusiastically before checking whether the change is correct.
Implementing only clear items while leaving related unclear items unresolved.
Adding professional-sounding features that no current caller needs.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps AI coding agents evaluate review feedback before making code changes.

faq.question: Does it automatically apply review comments?
faq.answer: No. It provides a decision workflow for understanding, verification, response, and implementation.

faq.question: Can it help with unclear feedback?
faq.answer: Yes. It tells the agent to ask clarifying questions before starting related work.

faq.question: Can it push back on a reviewer?
faq.answer: Yes. It encourages concise technical pushback when a suggestion is wrong or risky.

faq.question: Does it work with GitHub review threads?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance to reply in inline review threads instead of top-level comments.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
